sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The player moved from [27, 18] to [24, 18] after UP - that's 3 cells up. But this is NOT on the yellow cross! The yellow cross is at col 21 (vertical) and row 27 (horizontal). The player is now at [24, 18], which is off the cross entirely. This means the player can move freely in any direction, not just along the cross arms. The cross arms are just visual elements.
So the player moves 3 cells in the direction pressed. Let me think about this differently. The player can move anywhere on the board in steps of 3. The goal is probably to collect the boxes by moving onto them or near them and pressing SPACE.
Let me try to navigate to one of the boxes. The boxes are 2x2 with 1-pixel centers. Let me target one of the yellow boxes near the yellow cross.
Box at [8, 23]-[10, 25] has center at [9, 24]. Let me try to get close to it.
Current position: [24, 18]. To get to [9, 24], I need to go up 15 and right 6. That's 5 UP and 2 RIGHT.
Let me try moving to a box and pressing SPACE.

sections.content:

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player at [24, 18], moves 3 cells per step in any direction
# Can move freely, not constrained to cross arms
# Let me navigate to a box and try SPACE
# Box center at [9, 24] - need to go up 15 (5 UP) and right 6 (2 RIGHT)
action([('UP'), ('UP'), ('UP'), ('UP'), ('UP'), ('RIGHT'), ('RIGHT')])
</parameter>

</tool_call>
